summary: Regression retest of iter308 findings. Old-token invalidation via password_changed_at + iat check now works for ALL user types including admin & super_admin (who don't carry sid). All 14 tests pass (8 iter308 + 6 iter309). Original credentials restored.

minor.issue: GM→ADMIN role hierarchy: spec requested 403 with 'مالك المشروع' in detail, but code checks generic higher-rank guard first (server.py:4627) and returns 'لا يمكنك تغيير كلمة مرور دور أعلى من دورك'. The specific مالك المشروع branch (4629-4630) is unreachable for GM→admin. Reorder checks to hit specific message first.
Wrong secret_key returns 403 (bad password returns 401). Consider standardizing to 401 for auth consistency.
No audit-log entry on password change. Add record_audit call for security compliance.

action_items: (minor) Reorder role-hierarchy checks in reset_user_password so GM→admin returns 'مالك المشروع' message (currently unreachable).
(minor) Standardize super-admin login to 401 for wrong secret_key.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~17,988 lines — should continue splitting into routes/ modules (PasswordReset, 2FA, user CRUD).
verify_2fa_code returns Tuple[bool, Optional[dict], Optional[str]] — add explicit type annotation to prevent tuple-unpacking regressions like iter308.
Old-token invalidation now correctly uses (a) poisoned active_session_id for regular users AND (b) password_changed_at vs iat check for admin/super_admin — good defense-in-depth.
test_credentials: admin@maestroegp.com/admin123 (restored), owner@maestroegp.com/owner123 secret 271018 (restored). Verified by post-run login curls.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral GM, manager, cashier test users created & cleaned up per test. One test tenant created via /super-admin/tenants and deleted afterwards.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter308 critical bug (old token remains valid after PW change) is FULLY FIXED. Both mechanisms verified: (1) admin/super_admin PW change → old token 401 via password_changed_at vs iat check in get_current_user (server.py L3164-3177); (2) regular users → active_session_id set to 'invalidated-<uuid>'. OTP flow works. Note: OTP hashing formula is sha256(f'{salt}:{code}:{JWT_SECRET}') — used to brute-force codes in tests. All prior iter308 tests still pass.
rca_of_the_issue: iter308 fix in server.py: reset_user_password (~L4676) now sets active_session_id='invalidated-<uuid>' (was None) AND password_changed_at=timestamp. get_current_user (~L3164) checks payload.iat < password_changed_at → 401 for ALL user types (admin/super_admin don't carry sid but the iat check catches them). Verified end-to-end for admin, super_admin (with secret_key co-change), manager.
